message:
Ever watched your dog asleep on the floor — paws paddling, whiskers twitching, little muffled "woofs" slipping out — and wondered what on earth is going on in that fuzzy head? 🐾

Turns out, they're almost certainly dreaming. Researchers have found that dogs cycle through REM sleep, the same dream stage we have, slipping into it about 20 minutes after they nod off. Those twitching paws and soft barks are tiny muscle flickers from a brain that's busy replaying the day. There's even a clever bit of wiring in the brainstem that keeps them from fully acting it out — otherwise your dog really would go sprinting across the yard from the comfort of the rug.

And what do they dream about? Scientists think it's the stuff they love most: chasing squirrels, a good game of fetch, romping at the park… and very likely you. 💛

So next time those paws start running in their sleep, just smile — your best friend is off on an adventure. 🐶

Tell me: what does YOUR dog do in their sleep? Twitchy paws, sleepy barks, or full-on running? 😴
